news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a surge with institutional demand and strong ETF inflows driving prices upwards. Retail participation remains low, potentially leading to further price gains. Notable price gains include: Bitcoin at $111,752.97 (+4.5%), Ethereum at $2,654.73 (+7.6%), Solana at $178.90 (+6.7%), XRP at $2.42 (+2.9%), Dogecoin at $0.2411 (+6.6%), and Shiba Inu at $0.00001528 (+5.4%). Key statistics reveal a 22.1% decrease in large transaction volumes, a 7.7% increase in daily active addresses, a notable rise in transactions over $100,000 from 11,939 to 14,030 in a day, and a 332.7% increase in exchanges netflows. Coinglass data reports 98,027 traders liquidated in 24 hours for $328.86 million. Significant developments include Cardano's surge amid $600M ADA misuse allegations, Kraken's plan to launch 24/7 tokenized stock trading for global users via Solana, and industry experts like Anthony Scaramucci attributing Bitcoin's rise to a thin supply. Unicoin CEO Alex Konanykhin accused SEC of fraud. (Word count: 127)
news.headline: Bitcoin surpasses $111,000, Ethereum gains 7.6%, XRP +2.9%, Dogecoin +6.6%, as institutional demand drives market surge.

news.summary: Rayonier Advanced Materials Inc. (NYSE: RYAM) revealed that its senior management will be present at two upcoming equity conferences in Boston, MA. The KeyBanc Capital Markets Industrials & Basic Materials Conference will feature Joshua Hicks, the Senior Vice President of High Purity Cellulose, on May 29, 2025. The event will be held at the InterContinental Boston on 510 Atlantic Ave. Additionally, there will be opportunities for 1x1 meetings with attendees.
news.headline: RYAM's Senior VP to present at KeyBanc Capital Markets Industrials & Materials Conference on May 29, 2025, in Boston.

news.summary: In Toronto last week, Canadian Hearing Services (CHS) engaged in negotiations during a province-wide strike affecting numerous Deaf, deafblind, and hard of hearing Ontarians. CHS did not approach the table to negotiate, but rather to insist on compliance and quiet submission. They indicated that they would only present a settlement offer for the month-long strike if specific conditions were complied with, such as halting a campaign drawing attention to CHS's detachment from the deaf community. This strike impacts thousands of individuals in Ontario.
news.headline: CUPE 2073 is holding a press conference at Queen’s Park on Monday as CHS refuses to bargain amid a province-wide strike impacting Deaf Ontarians.

news.summary: In Braddock, Pennsylvania, the Braddock Carnegie Library Association (BCLA), along with U. S. Steel and the Pittsburgh Penguins, held a grand reopening event for the Braddock Carnegie Library on May 22, 2025. This celebration marked the completion of a four-year renovation of the oldest Carnegie Library in the U.S., a recognized National Historic Landmark. The reopening signifies a fresh start for the institution, serving residents for over three centuries.
news.headline: Braddock Carnegie Library reopens after 4-year renovation. Hosted by BCLA, U. S. Steel & Penguins, celebration on May 22, 2025.

news.summary: In Saint Louis on May 22, 2025, LiftFund, a nonprofit organization located in San Antonio, Texas, announced support for small businesses affected by recent tornadoes in Kentucky and Missouri. The organization is providing a loan program at a five percent interest rate through the Heartland initiative.
news.headline: LiftFund offers 5% interest Heartland Relief Loans to aid small businesses hit by May 19 tornadoes in KY & MO.

news.summary: The decentralized crypto exchange Cetus, running on the Sui blockchain, reported a hack on May 22 resulting in the theft of over $220 million. They have frozen $162 million of the stolen funds and are collaborating with the Sui Foundation and other entities to recover the rest. The foundation stated that validators have identified the addresses with the stolen funds and are freezing transactions from those addresses. $63 million of the stolen funds were transferred to the Ethereum network, with $53 million laundered through a particular wallet address. The hack reflects ongoing cybersecurity concerns in the crypto industry, prompting calls for increased security measures to avoid regulatory scrutiny. The response to the recovery efforts varied within the crypto community, with some expressing concerns about the network's decentralization. A separate incident involving a $38 million seizure from the Bybit hack-linked eXch exchange in Germany emphasizes the persistent threat of cyber incidents in the crypto space.
news.headline: Sui validators freeze $162M of $220M stolen in Cetus hack. Recovery efforts ongoing as industry grapples with cyber threats.

news.summary: Toll Brothers, Inc. revealed its plan to introduce Toll Brothers at HighPoint, a new upscale community located in Scottsdale, Arizona. This exclusive gated neighborhood will consist of 122 large home sites, providing a harmonious mix of luxury and tranquility amidst the elegant desert landscape. The development is currently in the site work phase at 39977 N. 115th Street in Scottsdale, with sales expected to commence in late summer 2025.
news.headline: Toll Brothers to open 122 luxury homes at HighPoint, Scottsdale in late summer 2025, featuring exclusive gated community.

news.summary: In the recent update, Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (NYSE: WSM) saw a significant drop in its shares following the announcement of its fiscal first quarter results for 2025. The company reported a revenue of $1.73 billion, marking a 4.2% increase compared to the same period last year, surpassing Wall Street's projected $1.67 billion. Comparable brand revenue demonstrated a 3.4% rise year over year. The GAAP earnings per share stood at $1.85, exceeding the analyst consensus of $1.77. However, the gross margin for Williams-Sonoma was reported at 44.3%, a decrease of 360 basis points from the previous year. This decline includes a 300-basis-point advantage from a freight adjustment in the same quarter of 2024. Furthermore, the adjusted gross margin witnessed a 60-basis-point decrease year over year, mainly due to a 220-basis-point reduction in merchandise margins, partially offset by 120 basis points. For more detailed information, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Williams-Sonoma absorbs tariff costs, reports $1.73B revenue (4.2% YoY increase), $1.85 EPS (beat $1.77 estimate), 44.3% gross margin drop.

news.summary: Buckle (NYSE: BKE) is set to announce its latest quarterly earnings on May 23, 2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of $0.66. Investors are looking for the company to beat this estimate and provide positive guidance for the next quarter. In the previous quarter, Buckle exceeded EPS expectations by $0.09, resulting in a 0.0% share price drop. Over the last year, Buckle's shares have risen by 12.03%. Understanding market sentiments and expectations is crucial for investors. The company has received one rating from analysts. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Buckle to announce Q1 earnings, analysts predict $0.66 EPS. Past EPS beats reflected in stable share prices. Stock up 12.03% in the last 52 weeks, investors remain optimistic. News details on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the global smartphone market saw a significant decline in sales in the first quarter of 2020. Total smartphone shipments fell by 13% compared to the same period last year, with a total of 275 million units shipped. The top three smartphone manufacturers – Samsung, Huawei, and Apple – all experienced a decline in sales, with Samsung maintaining the top spot with a 21% market share. This drop in sales is attributed to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, which has disrupted global supply chains and led to a decrease in consumer demand for smartphones. Industry analysts predict that the smartphone market will continue to face challenges in the coming months as the economic impact of the pandemic persists.
news.headline: Fannie and Freddie stocks skyrocket after Trump suggests privatization, hitting all-time highs.

news.summary: Globex Mining Enterprises Inc. announced that Radisson Mining Resources Inc. is expanding their 22,000-meter 2025 drill program by an additional 18,000 meters. The drilling will concentrate on the O’Brien Mine and East O’Brien area of the New Mineral Resource, reaching a depth of up to 2 kilometers. Globex maintains a 2% Net Smelter Royalty on the Kewagama Gold Mine section in the East O’Brien area. Previous and ongoing drilling has encountered high-grade gold values and visible gold. Visual free gold was observed in Radisson’s recent press release on May 21, 2025, highlighting holes OB-24-363 and OB24-361.
news.headline: Globex Mining reveals more visible gold on Kewagama Royalty Claims, Radisson expands 2025 drill program by 18,000 meters.

news.summary: Rio Tinto has been selected as the preferred partner for the Salares Altoandinos lithium project in Chile's Atacama region by state-owned mining company ENAMI. This partnership signifies a significant step forward in the lithium sector, with large reserves in the region.
news.headline: Rio Tinto chosen as partner for Salares Altoandinos lithium project in Chile by ENAMI. Project has world-class potential.

news.summary: Falls continue to be the primary cause of death within the construction sector, despite being one of the most avoidable risks. With this in mind, Sam Boak, President of Boak & Sons, has taken an initiative to enhance safety measures across the industry. Boak has extended an invitation to host the 2025 Stand-Down for Safety event organized by the Ohio Bureau of Workers Compensation. This move aims to tackle the issue of fall-related fatalities in the sector, which account for a significant portion of workplace deaths. The event is expected to gather a large number of industry professionals to raise awareness and implement strategies to prevent such incidents in the future.
news.headline: Sam Boak hosts 2025 "Stand-Down for Safety" event to promote industry-wide safety and prevent falls in construction.

news.summary: In recent findings, it has been highlighted that a personalized titanium implant has the potential to enhance functionality and overall well-being following surgery for maxillary cancer. This innovative approach serves as a viable solution for individuals who are not suitable candidates for conventional implants. The study, conducted in GLEN ALLEN, Virginia, on May 22, 2025, reveals noteworthy outcomes for patients, showcasing substantial improvements in their quality of life post-surgery. Published in the Journal of Oral Implantology, this report signifies a significant advancement in the field, demonstrating a promising alternative with key benefits for those undergoing maxillary cancer treatment.
news.headline: Custom titanium implant improves quality of life for maxillary cancer patients. Journal of Oral Implantology article from May 22, 2025.

news.summary: On May 22, 2025, in Scottsdale, Arizona, Meritage Homes Corporation, the fifth-largest homebuilder in the U.S., publicly revealed that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $0.43 per share. This dividend will be disbursed on June 30, 2025, to shareholders who are recorded as of June 16, 2025, at the market's closing.
